tr.td: Take 1 tablet(s) every day by oral route.

Take 1 tablet(s) twice a day by oral route.

Take 1 tablet(s) 3 times a day by oral route.
Take 1 tablet(s) every day by oral route.
Take 1 tablet(s) twice a day by oral route.
24 HR Diltiazem Hydrochloride 180 MG Extended Release Oral Capsule
24 HR Diltiazem Hydrochloride 180 MG Extended Release Oral Capsule
Take 2 capsule(s) every day by oral route.
Take 1 tablet(s) twice a day by oral route.
Take 1 tablet(s) every day by oral route.
Medication Note: taking 1 tablet three times daily
Take 1 tablet(s) twice a day by oral route.
Take 1 tablet(s) every day by oral route.
Medication Note: as needed for SBP >160 or DBP >100
Take 1 tablet(s) every day by oral route.
1 tab, tablet PO, BID, PRN as needed for anxiety, # 45 tab, Refill(s): 0
Take 2 tablet(s) twice a day by oral route.
Medication Note: Take 2 tablets twice daily for 3 weeks, then 1 tablet twice daily for 1 month, then 1 tablet once daily after that
metoprolol succinate ER 100 mg oral tablet, extended release

code.displayName: Relevant diagnostic tests and/or laboratory data

tr.td: Cholesterol Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 2093-3)
Triglyceride Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 2571-8)
Cholesterol.in HDL Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 2085-9)
Cholesterol.total/Cholesterol.in HDL Ser/Plas MRto Pt Qn (test code = 9830-1)
CHOLESTEROL/HDL INTERPRETATION: <4.0 = DECREASED CORONARY RISK 4.1-5.9 = AVERAGE CORONARY RISK >6.0 = INCREASED CORONARY RISK
Cholesterol.in LDL Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 2089-1)
The equation being used in this calculation is LDL=CHOLESTEROL-(HDL+(TRIGLYDERID E/5))
Glucose BldC MCnc Pt Qn Glucometer (test code = 41653-7)
Sodium Ser/Plas SCnc Pt Qn (test code = 2951-2)
Potassium Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 22760-3)
Chloride Ser/Plas SCnc Pt Qn (test code = 2075-0)
Carbon dioxide Ser/Plas SCnc Pt Qn (test code = 2028-9)
Glucose Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 2345-7)
Urea nitrogen Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 3094-0)
Creatinine Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 2160-0)
Calcium Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 17861-6)
Creatinine renal clearance Urine+Ser/Plas VRat Qn (test code = 33558-8)

This Creatinine Clearance calculation is an estimate for classification purposes only and should not be the only factor used in patient management decisions. T he Creatinine Clearance is a calculation valid for adults over 18 years with ad juGHMents for age, sex and weight. The calculation is not valid for patients wi th changing renal function or patients on dialysis. OBESITY WT. ADJUGHMENT USED
Glomerular filtration rate/1.73 sq M.predicted Ser/Plas ArVRat Pt Qn Creatinine-based formula (MDRD) (test code = 33914-3)

The GFR is a calculation valid for adults 18 to 70 years with adjuGHMents for ag e, sex and race. The calculation is not valid for patients on dialysis or with rapidly changing kidney function. Patients with GFR less than 60 are defined as having chronic kidney disease. A grading system of decreased kidney function h as been proposed: 90 - 60 mild, 59 -30 moderate, 29 -15 severe.
Glucose BldC MCnc Pt Qn Glucometer (test code = 41653-7)

Calcium Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 17861-6)
Creatinine renal clearance Urine+Ser/Plas VRat Qn (test code = 33558-8)

This Creatinine Clearance calculation is an estimate for classification purposes only and should not be the only factor used in patient management decisions. T he Creatinine Clearance is a calculation valid for adults over 18 years with ad juGHMents for age, sex and weight. The calculation is not valid for patients wi th changing renal function or patients on dialysis. OBESITY WT. ADJUGHMENT USED
Glomerular filtration rate/1.73 sq M.predicted Ser/Plas ArVRat Pt Qn Creatinine-based formula (MDRD) (test code = 33914-3)

The GFR is a calculation valid for adults 18 to 70 years with adjuGHMents for ag e, sex and race. The calculation is not valid for patients on dialysis or with rapidly changing kidney function. Patients with GFR less than 60 are defined as having chronic kidney disease. A grading system of decreased kidney function h as been proposed: 90 - 60 mild, 59 -30 moderate, 29 -15 severe.

INTERPRETIVE INFORMATION: Aldosterone, Serum Reference intervals for age 15 and older: Upright ......... 4.0 - 31.0 ng/dL Supine .......... Less than or equal to 16.0 ng/dL Unspecified ..... Less than or equal to 31.0 ng/dL Normal serum levels of aldosterone are dependent on the sodium intake and whether the patient is upright or supine. High sodium intake will tend to suppress serum aldosterone, whereas low sodium intake will elevate serum aldosterone. The reference intervals for serum aldosterone are based on normal sodium intake.
Access complete set of age- and/or gender-specific reference intervals for this test in the ARUP Laboratory Test Directory (aruplab.com). Performed by ARUP Laboratories, 500 Chipeta Way, SLC,UT 84108 800-522-2787 www.aruplab.com, Jerry W. Hussong, MD - Lab. Director
Glucose BldC MCnc Pt Qn Glucometer (test code = 41653-7)
Glucose BldC MCnc Pt Qn Glucometer (test code = 41653-7)
Glucose BldC MCnc Pt Qn Glucometer (test code = 41653-7)
Glucose BldC MCnc Pt Qn Glucometer (test code = 41653-7)
Natriuretic peptide.B Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 30934-4)
BNP is not an accurate marker of heart failure severity in patients on sacubitri l/valsartan (Entresto).

Creatinine renal clearance Urine+Ser/Plas VRat Qn (test code = 33558-8)

This Creatinine Clearance calculation is an estimate for classification purposes only and should not be the only factor used in patient management decisions. T he Creatinine Clearance is a calculation valid for adults over 18 years with ad juGHMents for age, sex and weight. The calculation is not valid for patients wi th changing renal function or patients on dialysis. OBESITY WT. ADJUGHMENT USED
Glomerular filtration rate/1.73 sq M.predicted Ser/Plas ArVRat Pt Qn Creatinine-based formula (MDRD) (test code = 33914-3)

The GFR is a calculation valid for adults 18 to 70 years with adjuGHMents for ag e, sex and race. The calculation is not valid for patients on dialysis or with rapidly changing kidney function. Patients with GFR less than 60 are defined as having chronic kidney disease. A grading system of decreased kidney function h as been proposed: 90 - 60 mild, 59 -30 moderate, 29 -15 severe.
Glucose BldC MCnc Pt Qn Glucometer (test code = 41653-7)

INTERPRETIVE INFORMATION: Renin Activity Adult, Normal sodium diet: Supine ................. 0.2-1.6 ng/mL/hr Upright ................ 0.5-4.0 ng/mL/hr Children, Normal sodium diet, Supine: Newborn (1-7 days) ..... 2.0-35.0 ng/mL/hr Cord blood ............. 4.0-32.0 ng/mL/hr 1-12 mos ............... 2.4-37.0 ng/mL/hr 13 mos-3 yrs ........... 1.7-11.2 ng/mL/hr 4-5 yrs ................ 1.0- 6.5 ng/mL/hr 6-10 yrs ............... 0.5- 5.9 ng/mL/hr 11-15 yrs .............. 0.5- 3.3 ng/mL/hr Children, normal sodium diet, Upright: 0-3 yrs ................ Not Available 4-5 yrs ................ Less than or equal to 15 ng/mL/hr 6-10 yrs ............... Less than or equal to 17 ng/mL/hr 11-15 yrs .............. Less than or equal to 16 ng/mL/hr Plasma renin activity measures enzyme ability to convert angiotensinogen to angiotensin I and is limited by the availability of angiotensinogen. Plasma renin activity is not an accurate indicator of enzyme activity when angiotensinogen is decreased. Performed by ARUP Laboratories, 500 Chipeta Way, SLC,UT 84108 800-522-2787 www.aruplab.com, Jerry W. Hussong, MD - Lab. Director

Glucose BldC MCnc Pt Qn Glucometer (test code = 41653-7)
Amphetamines Urine Threshold Pt Ord Screen (test code = 19261-7)

Urine results generated by screening are for medical treatment only. Gas Chromat ography Mass spectrometry confirmation by a reference lab is available. positiv e threshold for Methamphetamine/Amphetamine is 1000 ng/mL.
Barbiturates Urine Threshold Pt Ord Screen (test code = 19270-8)

Urine results generateed by screen are for medical treatment only. Gas Chromato graphy Mass spectrometry confirmation by a reference lab is available. Positive threshold for Barbiturates is 300 ng/mL.
Benzodiazepines Urine Threshold Pt Ord Screen (test code = 14316-4)

Urine results generated by screening are for medical treatment only. Gas Chroma tography Mass spectrometry confirmation by a reference lab is available. Positi ve threshold for Benzodiazepine is 200 ng/mL.
Benzoylecgonine Meconium MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 8186-9)
Urine results generated by screening are for medical treatment only. Gas Chroma tography Mass spectrometry confirmation by a refernce lab is available. Positi ve threshold for Cocaine is 300 ng/mL.
Opiates Urine Threshold Pt Ord Screen (test code = 19295-5)
Urine results generated by screening are for mediacl treatment only. Gas Chroma tography Mass spectrometry confirmation by areference lab is available. Positiv e threshold for Opiates is 300 ng/mL.
Phencyclidine Meconium MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 8231-3)

Urine results generated by screening are for medical treatment only. Gas Chroma tography Mass spectrometry confirmation by a reference lab is available. Positi ve threshold for Phencyclidine is 25 ng/mL.
Urine results generated by screening are for medical treatment only. Gas Chroma tography Mass spectrometry confirmation by a reference lab is available. Positi ve threshold for THC is 50 ng/mL.
Methadone Urine Threshold Pt Ord Screen (test code = 19550-3)

Urine results generated by screening are for medical treatment only. Gas Chroma tography Mass spectrometry confirmation by a reference lab is available. Positi ve threshold for Methadone is 300 ng/mL.
Glucose BldC MCnc Pt Qn Glucometer (test code = 41653-7)
Glucose BldC MCnc Pt Qn Glucometer (test code = 41653-7)
Potassium Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 22760-3)
Glucose BldC MCnc Pt Qn Glucometer (test code = 41653-7)

PROCEDURE: US RENAL WITH DOPPLER
TECHNIQUE: Real-time sonography in multiple planes of the kidneys, ureters and urinary bladder was performed with image documentation. Duplex color flow Doppler imaging and spectral waveform analysis of the renal arteries and veins was employed. CPT 76770, 93976
HISTORY: Chest Pain + evaluate for renal artery stenosis. Accel HTN
COMPARISONS: None .
FINDINGS: Maximum aortic systolic velocity: 113.1 cm/sec.
RIGHT kidney: Appearance: Normal echotexture. No focal renal mass, calculus, or hydronephrosis. Length: 11.6 cm. Main Renal Artery PSV:97.7 - 132.1 cm/s, (normal is less than or = 180 cm/sec) Renal Artery/Aortic systolic ratio (RAR): 0.9-1.2 , (Normal is less than or = 3.5, greater than 3.5 = 60-99% stenosis) RI: 0.5-0.7, (Normal is less than or = 0.7) Systolic acceleration time: 0.04-0.05, (Normal is less than or = 0.07 sec) Systolic acceleration index: 540-700 (Normal is greater than or = 300 cm/sec/sec) Renal vein(s): Normal antegrade flow.
LEFT kidney: Appearance: Normal echotexture. No focal renal mass, calculus, or hydronephrosis. Length: 11.0 cm. Main Renal Artery PSV:44.8-95.8, (normal is less than or = 180 cm/sec) Renal Artery/Aortic systolic ratio (RAR): 0.4-0.9 , (Normal is less than or = 3.5, greater than 3.5 = 60-99% stenosis) RI: 0.7-0.8, (Normal is less than or = 0.7) Systolic acceleration time: 0.04-0.09, (Normal is less than or = 0.07 sec) Systolic acceleration index: 700-800 (Normal is greater than or = 300 cm/sec/sec) Renal vein(s): Normal antegrade flow.
Bladder: Normal.
IMPRESSION: 1. No evidence of renal artery stenosis. 2. Mildly elevated resistive indices within the lower pole LEFT kidney..
ws:GENERALBA162593
Electronically Signed By Manish Patel on 2017-01-14 08:17 CTZ Call me with questions at my direct line (615)-986-6401

Glucose BldC MCnc Pt Qn Glucometer (test code = 41653-7)

PROCEDURE: PORTABLE CHEST
TECHNIQUE: A portable AP chest radiograph was obtained at 1/13/2016 5:36 PM. CPT 71010
HISTORY: Chest Pain + Chest Pain
COMPARISONS: December 23, 2015.
FINDINGS:
Heart: Borderline cardiomegaly. Mediastinum/Vessels: Normal. Lungs/Pleural space: Normal. Bony thorax: No acute osseous abnormality. Life support devices: None.
IMPRESSION: 1. Borderline cardiomegaly without evidence of acute pulmonary abnormality. Stable mild RIGHT diaphragmatic elevation..
ws:PMRTNRDWIN70D62
Electronically Signed By JEFFREY THOMAS on 2017-01-13 17:53 CTZ Call me with questions at my direct line (615)555-1212

The GFR is a calculation valid for adults 18 to 70 years with adjuGHMents for ag e, sex and race. The calculation is not valid for patients on dialysis or with rapidly changing kidney function. Patients with GFR less than 60 are defined as having chronic kidney disease. A grading system of decreased kidney function h as been proposed: 90 - 60 mild, 59 -30 moderate, 29 -15 severe.
Troponin I.cardiac Bld MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 42757-5)
Cholesterol Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 2093-3)
Triglyceride Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 2571-8)
Cholesterol.in HDL Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 2085-9)
Cholesterol.total/Cholesterol.in HDL Ser/Plas MRto Pt Qn (test code = 9830-1)
CHOLESTEROL/HDL INTERPRETATION: <4.0 = DECREASED CORONARY RISK 4.1-5.9 = AVERAGE CORONARY RISK >6.0 = INCREASED CORONARY RISK
Cholesterol.in LDL Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 2089-1)
The equation being used in this calculation is LDL=CHOLESTEROL-(HDL+(TRIGLYDERID E/5))
Cholesterol Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 2093-3)
Triglyceride Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 2571-8)
Cholesterol.in HDL Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 2085-9)

CHOLESTEROL/HDL INTERPRETATION: <4.0 = DECREASED CORONARY RISK 4.1-5.9 = AVERAGE CORONARY RISK >6.0 = INCREASED CORONARY RISK
Cholesterol.in LDL Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 2089-1)
The equation being used in this calculation is LDL=CHOLESTEROL-(HDL+(TRIGLYDERID E/5))
Natriuretic peptide.B Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 30934-4)
BNP is not an accurate marker of heart failure severity in patients on sacubitri l/valsartan (Entresto).
Hemoglobin A1c Bld MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 41995-2)
Estimated average glucose Bld MCnc Pt Qn Estimated from glycated hemoglobin (test code = 27353-2)
Amphetamines Urine Threshold Pt Ord Screen (test code = 19261-7)

Urine results generated by screening are for medical treatment only. Gas Chromat ography Mass spectrometry confirmation by a reference lab is available. positiv e threshold for Methamphetamine/Amphetamine is 1000 ng/mL.
Barbiturates Urine Threshold Pt Ord Screen (test code = 19270-8)

Urine results generateed by screen are for medical treatment only. Gas Chromato graphy Mass spectrometry confirmation by a reference lab is available. Positive threshold for Barbiturates is 300 ng/mL.
Benzodiazepines Urine Threshold Pt Ord Screen (test code = 14316-4)

Urine results generated by screening are for medical treatment only. Gas Chroma tography Mass spectrometry confirmation by a reference lab is available. Positi ve threshold for Benzodiazepine is 200 ng/mL.
Benzoylecgonine Meconium MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 8186-9)
Urine results generated by screening are for medical treatment only. Gas Chroma tography Mass spectrometry confirmation by a refernce lab is available. Positi ve threshold for Cocaine is 300 ng/mL.
Opiates Urine Threshold Pt Ord Screen (test code = 19295-5)
Urine results generated by screening are for mediacl treatment only. Gas Chroma tography Mass spectrometry confirmation by areference lab is available. Positiv e threshold for Opiates is 300 ng/mL.
Phencyclidine Meconium MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 8231-3)

Urine results generated by screening are for medical treatment only. Gas Chroma tography Mass spectrometry confirmation by a reference lab is available. Positi ve threshold for Phencyclidine is 25 ng/mL.
Urine results generated by screening are for medical treatment only. Gas Chroma tography Mass spectrometry confirmation by a reference lab is available. Positi ve threshold for THC is 50 ng/mL.
Methadone Urine Threshold Pt Ord Screen (test code = 19550-3)

Urine results generated by screening are for medical treatment only. Gas Chroma tography Mass spectrometry confirmation by a reference lab is available. Positi ve threshold for Methadone is 300 ng/mL.
Troponin I.cardiac Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 10839-9)
Thyroxine.free Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 3024-7)
Thyrotropin Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 27975-2)
Natriuretic peptide.B Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 30934-4)
Reviewed BNP is not an accurate marker of heart failure severity in patients on sacubitri l/valsartan (Entresto).
Sodium Ser/Plas SCnc Pt Qn (test code = 2951-2)
Potassium Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 22760-3)
Chloride Ser/Plas SCnc Pt Qn (test code = 2075-0)
Carbon dioxide Ser/Plas SCnc Pt Qn (test code = 2028-9)
Glucose Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 2345-7)
Urea nitrogen Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 3094-0)
Creatinine Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 2160-0)
Calcium Ser/Plas MCnc Pt Qn (test code = 17861-6)
Creatinine renal clearance Urine+Ser/Plas VRat Qn (test code = 33558-8)

This Creatinine Clearance calculation is an estimate for classification purposes only and should not be the only factor used in patient management decisions. T he Creatinine Clearance is a calculation valid for adults over 18 years with ad juGHMents for age, sex and weight. The calculation is not valid for patients wi th changing renal function or patients on dialysis. OBESITY WT. ADJUGHMENT USED
Glomerular filtration rate/1.73 sq M.predicted Ser/Plas ArVRat Pt Qn Creatinine-based formula (MDRD) (test code = 33914-3)

The GFR is a calculation valid for adults 18 to 70 years with adjuGHMents for ag e, sex and race. The calculation is not valid for patients on dialysis or with rapidly changing kidney function. Patients with GFR less than 60 are defined as having chronic kidney disease. A grading system of decreased kidney function h as been proposed: 90 - 60 mild, 59 -30 moderate, 29 -15 severe.

PROCEDURE: PORTABLE CHEST
TECHNIQUE: A portable AP chest radiograph was obtained at 12/23/2015 1:28 AM. CPT 71010
HISTORY: Chest Pain + Arrhythmia/Palpitations
COMPARISONS: November 10, 2015.
FINDINGS:
Heart: Normal. Mediastinum/Vessels: Normal. Lungs/Pleural space: Normal. Bony thorax: No acute osseous abnormality. Life support devices: None.
IMPRESSION: 1. No active cardiopulmonary disease..
